prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging population and urbanization

riskFactors: Low calcium diet and low dairy intake
Darker skin pigmentation reducing vitamin D synthesis
vulnerablePopulations: Rural women with poor nutrition and low sunlight exposure
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: WHO DXA T-score ≤ -2.5, adapted with Indian ethnic-specific reference ranges (e.g., lower BMD thresholds)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: very low, <5% in public sector, limited DXA machines
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 10% poor due to limited screening and awareness

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: DXA when available, FRAX India tool, clinical assessment common in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Calcium + vitamin D supplementation, bisphosphonates, lifestyle counseling; Ayurvedic calcium sources (e.g., kukkutandatvak bhasma)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Highest burden globally at younger ages (mean onset 10 years earlier), severe vitamin D deficiency (>70% population), limited DXA access, low dairy consumption, early menopause, skin pigmentation reducing vitamin D synthesis
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness via IOF India campaigns, affordable generic bisphosphonates, Ayurvedic supplements
